data.title: TINC (ENXTBR:TINC) One Off €13.6M Loss Tests High 62.5% Net Margin Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

TINC (ENXTBR:TINC) posts FY 2025 first half results

TINC (ENXTBR:TINC) has released its FY 2025 first half numbers, reporting revenue of €32.6 million and EPS of €0.50. Over the recent periods, revenue moved from €34.7 million and EPS of €0.50 in FY 2024 H1 to €37.1 million and EPS of €0.67 in FY 2024 H2, before landing at the current €32.6 million and EPS of €0.50. Investors will be weighing these figures against a backdrop of forecast revenue and earnings growth. With net profit margins sitting high on a trailing 12 month basis, the focus now is on how durable that profitability looks as the growth and risk trade off comes into clearer view.

See our full analysis for TINC.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings print lines up with the widely shared narratives about TINC, and where the data challenges or supports those stories.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

62.5% net margin keeps profitability high

  • Over the last 12 months, TINC reported net income of €40.6 million on revenue of €65.0 million, which works out to a net margin of 62.5% compared with 59.2% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that this margin strength sits alongside FY 2025 H1 net income of €18.4 million on €32.6 million of revenue, and trailing 12 month EPS of €0.95 to €1.17. This supports the idea of a high margin, asset backed model even as individual half year numbers move around.
    • Supporters who focus on infrastructure and real asset exposure as relatively steady can point to margins above 60% on trailing revenue between €64.9 million and €71.8 million as evidence that profitability is not just a one off spike.
    • At the same time, the dip from €24.5 million net income in FY 2024 H2 to €18.4 million in FY 2025 H1 shows that even with strong margins, reported profit can shift between periods, so the bullish case has to lean on the longer trailing record rather than a single half.

EPS trend and one off loss add complexity

  • EPS for the individual halves sits at €0.50 in FY 2024 H1, €0.67 in FY 2024 H2 and €0.50 in FY 2025 H1, while trailing 12 month EPS across the last four snapshots ranges from €0.95 to €1.17. Those trailing figures also include a one off loss of €13.6 million in the last 12 months to 31 Dec 2025.
  • Critics with a bearish tilt highlight that earnings were negative over the most recent 12 month window and were hit by the €13.6 million one off loss, which makes them question how reliable the trailing EPS band of €0.95 to €1.17 really is as a guide.
    • They can point to the step down in net income from €24.5 million in FY 2024 H2 to €18.4 million in FY 2025 H1, and to the swing from positive trailing EPS of €1.17 a year ago to negative reported earnings over the latest 12 months, as signs that earnings can be volatile when one off items hit.
    • On the other hand, the fact that underlying net income, excluding extra items, still totals €40.6 million on €65.0 million of revenue over the latest 12 months provides a counterpoint, suggesting that the bearish focus on the one off loss does not fully capture the underlying profitability that the income statement is showing.
A sharp one off loss changing the earnings picture while margins stay above 60% is exactly the kind of twist that long term bulls and short term bears will argue over, so it can be useful to see how each side frames TINC's story before you decide what matters most to you. 🐻 TINC Bear Case

Share price, P/E of 13.4x and DCF fair value

  • With the share price at €11.24, the stock trades on a P/E of 13.4x versus peer and European Capital Markets industry averages of 22.1x and 15.4x, and sits about 2.6% below a DCF fair value of €11.54.
  • Supportive investors argue that this combination of a P/E below both peers and the broader industry, plus a price slightly under the €11.54 DCF fair value, makes TINC look relatively attractively valued, but the facts also show trade offs, including a 5.16% dividend that is not comfortably covered by free cash flow and substantial shareholder dilution over the past year.
    • The lower P/E compared with peers and the sector ties in with the view of TINC as an income oriented, asset backed name where investors are paying less for each euro of trailing earnings than in many comparable stocks.
    • At the same time, the weak free cash flow coverage of that 5.16% yield and the dilution that existing holders faced are concrete data points that may explain why the market is not assigning a higher multiple even thoughNext Steps

Quanta Services (PWR) is back in focus after its latest earnings release topped expectations, accompanied by 2026 guidance that points to continued growth and a record backlog tied to grid modernization work.

See our latest analysis for Quanta Services.

Despite a 1 day share price return of a 1.39% decline to US$559.02, Quanta Services has a 90 day share price return of 27.6% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 114.59%. This points to momentum that has built around recent earnings beats, record grid focused backlog and upcoming Investor Day updates.

If this grid modernization story has you thinking bigger about infrastructure opportunities, take a look at our 23 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks sourced from the Simply Wall St screener.

With Quanta already trading near some analyst targets and carrying an intrinsic value estimate at a premium to the current US$559 price, is there still a reasonable entry point here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 2.4% Overvalued

According to Vestra, the most followed narrative pegs Quanta Services fair value at $546, slightly below the last close of $559.02, which tightens the margin for error around new buyers.

The fair value for Quanta Services (PWR) is calculated using my fair value method by applying a 42x Forward P/E multiple to the 2026 Adjusted EPS midpoint of $13.00, reflecting its dominant market share and the visibility of its $44 billion backlog. This results in a fair value of $546.00 in local currency (USD). At the current price of $540.19, the stock is trading almost exactly at its intrinsic value, with a marginal 1.1% discount.
